_README: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for every AI model ID this project uses (now + planned).
Goal: when a newer/better model ships, change the 'id' here in ONE place.
'active'  = wired into a script TODAY. 'usedBy' lists the file(s).
'planned' = not built yet; a future capability will read this.
IMPORTANT (honest note): the 'active' scripts currently HARDCODE these IDs.
Editing this file does NOT yet change their behavior on its own — EXCEPT where 'envOverride' is set
(e.g. set the GEMINI_MODEL env var to swap Gemini without touching code).
Wiring every capability to READ this file is a planned refactor.
Until then, to change an 'active' model: set its envOverride if it has one, OR edit this file AND the 'usedBy' script(s).
GOVERNING RULE: the video 'visual cortex' is gemini-3.1-flash-lite — never substitute Gemini 2.5.
VFX POLICY: NO local/free VFX models. Local matting/depth/optical-flow/upscale/inpaint models and
local-free generators are not a supported path (consumer GPUs can't run them reliably).
Generative video = PAID cloud, Runway + Veo + Seedance ONLY (no Kling/Pika/Luma/Sora). VFX otherwise = deterministic Remotion-native effects (free, code-it).
NOTE: non-model acquisition TOOLS (yt-dlp for web/social video download, acquire/) are pinned in capabilities/requirements.txt, not here — this file is model IDs only.

NOTE: screen-recording TOOLS (screen-record/) are NOT models either — Playwright is a browser-automation TOOL. Pin the `playwright` library in package.json devDeps (>=1.59, the page.screencast version) and `@playwright/mcp` (pinned EXACT — it iterates daily, NEVER @latest) in .mcp.json. See the _tools note for the pinned versions; doctor checks their presence.

_tools._note: Non-model TOOLS the capability layer depends on (browser automation, downloaders). Pinned in package.json / .mcp.json / requirements.txt — recorded here so the 'edit one place to bump' discipline extends to tools.

playwright.purpose: screen-record/ deterministic capture — the page.screencast({onFrame}) API ships in 1.59.
playwright.status: pinned (on-demand install: npm i -D playwright && npx playwright install chromium; doctor yellows if absent)

playwrightMcp.purpose: screen-record/ EXPLORE stage — a11y-tree browser automation + --codegen.
playwrightMcp.status: pinned (.mcp.json present; npx-resolved on first use)

_meta.note: Active IDs verified against the capability scripts. Local/free VFX model sections (matting/depth/opticalFlow/upscale/inpaint + localFree generators) are intentionally absent per the VFX policy above.

visualCortex.purpose: Video 'eyes': describe + QA + scene/lighting analysis + bounding-box detection (auto-rotoscoping) + reference-video deconstruction into a style-spec (via the council). The QA/verifier model.
visualCortex.notes: NEVER swap to gemini-2.5-*. Flash-lite over-reads — ground verdicts in objective signals.
alternatives: gemini-3.1-pro (only for a single bbox frame if flash-lite boxes are weak)

cloud.purpose: Word-level captions / ground-truth word timing (the 'ears'). OpenAI's hosted Whisper API model name.
cloud.rule: STT is OpenAI whisper-1 ONLY — NO local/faster-whisper STT path or fallback. Do not re-introduce local STT.

tts.purpose: Default TTS model — stable, 29 languages, deterministic-friendly.
alternatives: eleven_v3 (most expressive, 74 langs, audio tags work; --v3)
tts._cost: ESTIMATE for the budget ledger only — ElevenLabs bills in CREDITS whose USD rate varies by plan (~$0.18/1k chars on Creator). The plan-gate cost approval is the real spend control.

ttsExpressive.purpose: Expressive/emotional TTS — required for audio tags ([excited], [whispers], ...). Selected via --v3.

music._cost: ESTIMATE for the budget ledger (ElevenLabs credits → USD varies by plan).

sfx._cost: ESTIMATE for the budget ledger (short clips; ElevenLabs credits → USD varies by plan).

voiceId_default.purpose: The default TTS voice — INTENTIONALLY EMPTY. Set YOUR voice in brand/brand.json → voice.elevenlabsVoiceId (a cloned voice, or pick any at https://elevenlabs.io/voices); elevenlabs-tts.ts reads it from there (brand.json is the config boundary, not this file).
voiceId_default.notes: This is a VOICE id, not a model id. Instant Voice Cloning requires ElevenLabs Starter tier+ for API use (PAYG returns 401 ivc_not_permitted).

thumbnail.purpose: Video thumbnails: a frame screenshot from the finished video + a text prompt → polished thumbnail in the video's aspect ratio, via /v1/images/edits. Identity/face preservation is automatic (the model always runs input images at high fidelity).
thumbnail._cost: ESTIMATE for the budget ledger (~$0.04–0.21/image by size/quality + input-frame tokens; using the high-quality mid).
thumbnail.notes: Pin the dated snapshot, not the 'gpt-image-2' alias. NEVER send input_fidelity (removed in gpt-image-2 — the request fails). Sizes: edges multiples of 16, long edge ≤3840, ratio ≤3:1; we generate at long-edge-2048 (16:9 → 2048x1152, 9:16 → 1152x2048) and downscale. Cost ≈ $0.04–0.21/image (medium–high) + input-frame tokens. Needs openai npm >6.34.0 (images.edit validator bug); we ship 6.37.0. Non-ASCII glyphs (æ/ø/å …) may garble in-model — overlay text in Remotion instead. Prompting rules: capabilities/generate/THUMBNAIL-GUIDE.md.

mlGrade.provider: ICCV 2025 (Shin et al., arXiv:2508.00548) — local PyTorch
mlGrade.purpose: Reference-image -> generated .cube LUT (diffusion); apply via FFmpeg lut3d. Heavy/optional.
mlGrade.notes: Checkpoints on Google Drive — pin a local mirror. Multi-sec/frame. Primary color path is deterministic FFmpeg lut3d, not this. (This is a COLOR model, not a VFX model — outside the no-local-VFX policy — but still deferred on-demand.)

generativeVideo._note: PAID CLOUD TRIO ONLY: Runway + Veo + Seedance. No local/free generator, no Kling/Pika/Luma/Sora. GENERATIVE IS A LAST RESORT: prefer deterministic Remotion-native effects (CSS/Tailwind/@remotion-*/GSAP/Three.js/Skia) — generative only for photoreal/organic content that can't be coded. All cloud + PAID; cost+approval gated by the APIBudgetGuard; async-poll; result URLs expire — download immediately. Prompting templates of record: capabilities/vfx/generate/templates/.
generativeVideo._cacheKeyRule: Runway: sha256({prompt, model, seed, ref_image_sha256}). Veo + Seedance: sha256({prompt, model, ref_image_sha256, durationSec, aspect, resolution}) — neither exposes a deterministic seed parameter.
generativeVideo._routerRules: Talking-head/identity-locked face → Veo 3.1 (NEVER Seedance 2.0, which blocks realistic faces). 9:16 vertical establishing plate → Veo 3.1. Mood/texture on black bg → Seedance 2.0. V2V relight/restyle → Runway Aleph. Rapid motion-iteration → Runway Gen-4 Turbo (5 credits/s, 2.5–3× faster than Gen-4.5).

veo.provider: Google Veo (via Gemini API key) — cloud, PAID
veo.purpose: High-fidelity cinematic generation + start/end-frame control + timestamp prompting + Extend (chain to ~140s) + native synchronized audio + negative_prompt support. Default for talking-head, 9:16 ads, identity-locked multi-shot. Uses existing GEMINI_API_KEY (billing-enabled).
veo.notes: PAID Standard tier ≈ $0.40/s. Veo 3.1 supports negative_prompt + audio_enabled + 'Ingredients to Video' character lock (3–4 ref images + repeat character descriptor word-for-word).

veoFast.provider: Google Veo Fast (via Gemini API key) — cloud, PAID
veoFast.purpose: Cheapest high-fidelity Veo tier — preferred for iteration drafts and longer sequences where Standard cost would blow the budget guard.
veoFast.notes: $0.15/s — same Gemini key as standard Veo. Use when drafting; promote to Standard for final.

runwayGen4_5.purpose: Premium realism + camera-language fidelity. Supports `seed` (the ONLY provider in the trio that does — full GenerationCache determinism). Gen-4 References (contact-sheet, neutral expression) for identity-lock close-ups.
runwayGen4_5.notes: 12 credits/s. IMAGE-TO-VIDEO RULE: motion-only text — STRIP visual descriptors (subject/composition/color) from prompt or motion degrades.

runwayGen4Turbo.provider: Runway Gen-4 Turbo (runwayml SDK) — cloud, PAID
runwayGen4Turbo.purpose: 2.5–3× faster, ~40% cost of Gen-4.5 — the router's iteration tier (draft motion language here, render final on Gen-4.5).
runwayGen4Turbo.notes: 5 credits/s. Same SDK + auth as Gen-4.5; choose via the `model` argument.

runwayAleph.purpose: Video-to-video relight/restyle/in-context edit — purpose-built for transforming EXISTING footage (poorly-lit source clips, sky swaps, lighting changes). ≤30s / 10 cuts per request.
runwayAleph.notes: Prompt rule: granular ('Change only the sky to sunset orange'), NEVER vague ('Make it look better'); always include 'Preserve [subject], [camera], [composition]'.

seedance2.provider: ByteDance Seedance 2.0 via fal.ai — cloud, PAID
seedance2.purpose: Lowest-cost generator — strongest CAMERA LANGUAGE; multimodal @Image1/@Video1/@Audio1 references (up to 9/3/3); 'Cut to' multi-shot. Default for mood/textural shots on black bg.
seedance2.notes: HARD RULES: (1) inject `cameraFixed:false` for ANY camera motion (default is locked → silent failure); (2) Seedance 2.0 BLOCKS realistic human faces — router MUST escalate to Veo 3.1 (or Seedance 1.5 Pro for lip-sync) when the brief involves a real person's face. NO seed parameter → cache key uses {prompt, model, ref_image_sha256, durationSec, aspect, resolution}.

seedance1_5Pro.provider: ByteDance Seedance 1.5 Pro via fal.ai — cloud, PAID
seedance1_5Pro.purpose: Lip-sync fallback when Seedance 2.0 blocks a human-face shot AND Veo 3.1 is unavailable / over budget. Verify pricing and availability.
seedance1_5Pro.notes: (verify) — pricing TBD; surfaced here so the router has a non-Veo fallback for lip-sync. Same fal.ai SDK + FAL_KEY.
